data.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Ă— THIS child 8937 "Producer<->consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), re-cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

CONFIRMS the prior G3 audit on this ticket. This child is the keystone and FULLY covers its owned criteria:
- Parent AC1 (SSOT snapshot-entry schema; producer output + consumer reads validate against it) â†’ 8937 AC1 (`_snapshot_schema.py` TypedDict + JSON Schema SSOT) + AC2 (fetch_snapshot validated vs schema; FAILS on pre-fix conditions â€” issuelinks absent / comment under wrong key). FULLY.
- Parent AC2 (one round-trip contract fetcherâ†’snapshotâ†’differsâ†’applier through the production path, using real-payload fixtures) â†’ 8937 AC3 (round-trip through production path; fails if a producer key/shape diverges from a consumer read). Fixtures correctly delegated to fe57 (depends_on declared). FULLY.
- Parent SC1 (producer/consumers can no longer drift on snapshot shape without a test failing) â†’ directly satisfied by the joint round-trip contract; reinforced by fe57 (honest fixtures/fake) and 59ef (verified-fake fidelity). FULLY.
- Contributes to SC5/AC7 via 8937 AC4 (semantic assertions; no whole-blob golden equality / no mock-call-count).

All parent criteria this child does NOT own are correctly delegated to named siblings: AC3/SC2 â†’ 33d0 (collapse appliers); AC4/SC3 â†’ 2359 (sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary); AC5/SC4 â†’ 822a (hermetic bidirectional round-trip + de-encode 'expected-bug' guards); fixtures/fake foundation â†’ fe57; verified-fake honesty â†’ 59ef. This child's declared OUT-of-scope items (fixturesâ†’fe57, applier collapseâ†’33d0, telemetryâ†’2359) are consistent with the roster. No per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to child 8937 itself for its owned criteria.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: AC1/AC2/SC1 FULLY covered by this child; SC5/AC7 contributed; AC3/AC4/AC5/SC2/SC3/SC4 covered by named siblings; AC7 structural/aggregate; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence, already recorded on this ticket â€” 8937 named as its natural home â€” and on siblings 33d0/59ef/822a/2359; NOT re-filed here to avoid duplication).

No NEW finding for this pairing. The single roster-level AC6 absence is already tracked on this ticket and across the sibling set.
